displayTitle: Choosing the Probe, and Defending It

beats.narration: At Level Two you don't just run the probe — you pick it and defend the pick. A flaw the probe can't see never becomes data.

payload.title: CHOOSE THE PROBE
payload.sub: A flaw the probe can't see never becomes data.

beats.narration: Every probe is three stacked decisions: where the coil sits, how it's wound and connected, and what physically senses the field.
Mode decides what reaches the screen. Absolute sees everything, including drift. Differential cancels what's shared, drawing two lobes on local flaws. Reflection splits driver from pickup.

payload.heading: Three modes, three signatures
payload.note: ABSOLUTE SEES ALL · DIFFERENTIAL CANCELS SHARED · REFLECTION SPLITS

beats.narration: And orientation can beat you. A bobbin's currents run circumferential, so circumferential cracks hide. The fix isn't a setting — it's a different coil.

payload.heading: Orientation problems need a new coil

beats.narration: Coils carry conventional work; as frequency falls toward DC, Hall sensors take over. Close every choice with configuration, mode, element — or you chose by habit.

payload.sub: Say all three, or the flaw didn't pick the probe.
